summary: Iteration 25 - Testing menu simplification, audit logs, and launch readiness PDF. Backend APIs 100% working (11/11 tests passed). Found critical bug: PDF export button broken due to auth method mismatch.

critical.issue: PDF endpoint only accepts Authorization Bearer header but frontend uses ?token= query param for window.open(). This breaks PDF export.

integration_issues.flow: PDF Export on Launch Readiness page
integration_issues.issue: handleExportPDF() uses window.open() with ?token= query param but backend doesn't read query param tokens. PDF download returns 401 Unauthorized.

categories_tested.Audit Log Endpoints: 6 tests - list logs, filters, entity logs, actions, entity-types
categories_tested.Launch Report Endpoints: 4 tests - auth checks, JSON endpoint, PDF endpoint
categories_tested.Existing Health Endpoints: 1 test - QA health check still working

frontend_ui_tests.sidebar_navigation: PASS - All 29 nav items found, grouped sections (Sales, Operations, Inventory, Finance, Marketing, Settings) working
frontend_ui_tests.audit_log_page: PASS - Filter inputs, Apply/Clear buttons, table display all working
frontend_ui_tests.launch_readiness_page: PASS - Score card, checks grid, data counts, manual checklist all rendering correctly
frontend_ui_tests.pdf_export_button: FAIL - Returns 401 due to auth mismatch

features_verified: Audit Log API (/api/admin/audit) - list and filter audit logs ✓
Audit Actions/Entity-Types APIs - distinct values for filtering ✓
Launch Report JSON API (/api/admin/launch-report/json) - returns readiness data ✓
Launch Report PDF API (/api/admin/launch-report/pdf) - PDF generation works with Bearer token ✓
Audit Log Page (/admin/audit) - filter inputs and table working ✓
Launch Readiness Page - shows score and checks ✓ (uses /api/admin/qa/health-check)
Admin sidebar with grouped navigation (SALES, OPERATIONS, INVENTORY, FINANCE, MARKETING, SETTINGS) ✓
action_items: FIX CRITICAL: Add query param token support to launch_readiness_report_routes.py get_admin_user() for PDF endpoint, OR change frontend to use fetch() with blob download instead of window.open()
MINOR: LaunchReadinessPage.jsx uses /api/admin/qa/health-check but PDF uses /api/admin/launch-report/json - consider syncing these endpoints for data consistency
critical_code_review_comments: PDF endpoint auth mismatch: Backend uses HTTPBearer (Authorization header) but frontend sends token as query param via window.open(). Common pattern is to support both header AND query param for PDF downloads.

Data source mismatch: LaunchReadinessPage.jsx line 12 uses /api/admin/qa/health-check for display but PDF export at line 26 uses /api/admin/launch-report/pdf which has different checks (has_company_name, has_tax_config, has_currency vs has_products, has_variants, etc.)

success_rate.frontend: 90% - all UI working except PDF export auth

mocked_apis_list: Resend email service - hook exists but not used

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 25 added audit log system and launch readiness PDF export. Backend APIs all working. Critical issue: PDF export button on /admin/launch-readiness returns 401 because frontend uses window.open() with ?token= query param but backend only reads Authorization header. Solution: Modify get_admin_user() in launch_readiness_report_routes.py to also check query params for 'token', or use fetch API with blob download on frontend.

rca_of_issue.root_cause: Frontend uses window.open(`${baseUrl}/api/admin/launch-report/pdf?token=${token}`) which can't set Authorization headers. Backend get_admin_user() only reads from HTTPBearer (Authorization header), not from query params.
rca_of_issue.suggested_fix: Option A: Modify backend get_admin_user() to also check request.query_params.get('token'). Option B: Change frontend to use fetch() API with Authorization header, then create blob URL for download.
